text:
ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Attending: [Attending Oncologist]
Department: Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Routine oncology follow-up; metastatic triple-negative breast cancer on olaparib (Lynparza). Approximately 6 months since olaparib initiation.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Okonkwo is a 39-year-old (turning 40) West African woman with a history of stage IIB left breast TNBC (diagnosed approximately 2 years prior), now with confirmed metastatic recurrence to bone (right 5th posterior rib, T10 vertebral body, right iliac wing) and liver (two right lobe lesions), diagnosed approximately 18 months after her definitive surgery. She presents today for follow-up at approximately 6 months of systemic therapy with olaparib 300 mg BID, initiated as first-line treatment for metastatic disease in the context of a confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.3750G>A, p.E1250*).

Brief disease and treatment timeline for context: She originally received neoadjuvant pembrolizumab (Keytruda) plus dose-dense AC (ddAC x4 cycles) followed by weekly paclitaxel x12 weeks with concurrent pembro, then underwent left lumpectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy and ALND. Surgical pathology demonstrated residual IDC 1.4 cm (ypT1c ypN0(i+), RCB-II, Miller-Payne grade 3). She completed adjuvant WBRT with regional nodal irradiation, then adjuvant capecitabine (Xeloda) x6 cycles (dose-reduced to 750 mg/m2 BID for HFS) with pembrolizumab maintenance to complete 17 total pembro cycles. She developed pembrolizumab-associated hypothyroidism during adjuvant therapy, currently managed with levothyroxine 50 mcg daily. Approximately 18 months post-surgery, she presented with right rib pain and was found to have multifocal osseous metastases and two small hepatic lesions on imaging, confirmed pathologically as metastatic TNBC (iliac wing biopsy: poorly differentiated carcinoma, ER/PR/HER2 negative, GATA3+, CK7+). NGS on the metastatic biopsy revealed the original driver alterations (TP53 p.R248W, BRCA1 p.E1250*, PTEN loss, RB1 p.Q670*) plus acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R and CDK4 amplification. PD-L1 CPS was 8 on the metastatic specimen. She received palliative RT to the symptomatic right 5th rib (20 Gy/5 fx) prior to starting olaparib. Zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg IV q4 weeks was also initiated concurrently.

At her 3-month restaging scan, she had a partial response in the liver (1.2 cm lesion decreased to 0.9 cm, 0.8 cm lesion decreased to 0.6 cm) with stable osseous disease. She is now presenting for the 6-month follow-up visit.

Today's visit: The patient reports overall tolerating olaparib reasonably well. She endorses persistent mild fatigue, which she rates as 3-4/10 in severity -- present since starting olaparib and consistent throughout, not worsening. She takes ondansetron (Zofran) 4 mg PRN with good effect for occasional mild nausea, predominantly in the mornings; she estimates she takes it approximately 3-4 times per week. She denies vomiting. No dyspnea, chest pain, or palpitations. She continues to have grade 1 peripheral sensory neuropathy in both feet (numbness, tingling), which she reports is stable and unchanged from prior assessments; she is still on duloxetine 60 mg daily. She reports that her right rib pain is well-controlled (1/10) since completing palliative radiation -- she has not required her breakthrough opioid for several weeks. She denies new bone pain. No headaches or neurologic symptoms to suggest CNS involvement. She is currently working part-time as a high school biology teacher, having reduced her schedule due to fatigue. She reports her mood is "okay," with some understandable anxiety about disease trajectory. She has spoken with gynecologic oncology about risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) given her germline BRCA1 mutation, and she has decided to proceed; a surgical date has been scheduled.

Her sister recently tested positive for the BRCA1 familial variant and has been referred for genetic risk management.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1, no fevers, no chills, no unintentional weight loss (weight stable per nursing triage).
- HEENT: No headache, no visual changes.
- Respiratory: No dyspnea, no cough.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
- GI: Mild intermittent nausea (grade 1), well-managed with PRN ondansetron. No vomiting, no diarrhea, no constipation, no abdominal pain.
- GU: No dysuria or hematuria. Menstrual periods ongoing (premenopausal).
- MSK: Residual right rib discomfort 1/10, improving. No new joint pain. Grade 1 bilateral foot neuropathy, stable.
- Skin: No new rash. No palmar-plantar erythema. Post-lumpectomy and post-RT left breast changes stable.
- Neuro: Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy bilateral feet as above. No dizziness or imbalance.
- Endo/other: Hypothyroidism, well-controlled on levothyroxine.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital signs: BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 76 bpm, RR 16, Temp 36.8°C, O2 sat 98% on room air. Weight: 67 kg (stable from last visit). BMI: 24.1 kg/m2.

General: Alert, well-appearing woman. No acute distress. Appears mildly fatigued but conversational and engaged.

HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx clear. No oral mucositis. No cervical lymphadenopathy. Mild residual diffuse alopecia (regrowth present, approximately 3 cm in length).

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No jugular venous distension.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, wheezes, or decreased breath sounds.

Breasts: Left breast with expected post-lumpectomy contour change and radiation-induced skin changes (mild hyperpigmentation, mild firmness in the upper outer quadrant corresponding to lumpectomy site). No palpable mass. No skin ulceration. Clip artifact palpable at lumpectomy bed. Right breast: no mass, no skin changes. No nipple discharge bilaterally.

Axillae: No palpable axillary lymphadenopathy bilaterally. Left axilla with expected post-ALND changes.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly on palpation (liver edge not appreciable). No splenomegaly. No palpable masses.

Musculoskeletal: Mild tenderness to firm palpation over the right 5th posterior rib (improved from prior). No midline spinal tenderness. Gait normal.

Neurologic: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout upper and lower extremities. Mild symmetric decreased sensation to light touch in bilateral feet (consistent with grade 1 sensory neuropathy, stable).

Skin: No rash. Surgical scar left breast and left axilla healing well.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (today's visit):
CBC:
  - WBC: 5.1 x10^9/L (within normal limits)
  - Hemoglobin: 10.2 g/dL (LOW; baseline prior to metastatic treatment was 12.8 g/dL; grade 1 anemia)
  - MCV: 98 fL (macrocytic; consistent with PARP inhibitor effect)
  - Platelets: 198 x10^9/L (within normal limits)
  - ANC: 2.4 x10^9/L (adequate)
  - Reticulocyte count: 1.1% (low-normal; relative reticulocytopenia in context of anemia)

Iron studies: Ferritin 68 ng/mL (normal). Transferrin saturation 24% (normal). Iron deficiency not a contributing factor.

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  - Na 138, K 4.0, Cl 101, CO2 25, BUN 14, Cr 0.72 mg/dL, eGFR >60
  - Glucose 88 mg/dL
  - ALT 28 U/L, AST 22 U/L, Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, Alkaline phosphatase 112 U/L (mildly elevated, likely related to osseous metastases)
  - Albumin 3.9 g/dL

Thyroid function: TSH 2.4 mIU/L (within normal limits, well-controlled on levothyroxine 50 mcg).

CA 27-29: Not routinely monitored per guidelines; not checked.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Most recent restaging scans (3-month assessment, approximately 3 months ago):
  - CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Right lobe hepatic lesions decreased compared to baseline -- previously 1.2 cm now 0.9 cm, previously 0.8 cm now 0.6 cm. No new liver lesions. No pulmonary nodules. No new lymphadenopathy. Right 5th rib with post-radiation changes, no new cortical disruption.
  - Bone scan: Previously identified foci at T10 and right iliac wing stable. No new sites of uptake.
  - Overall: Partial response in the liver, stable osseous disease.
Next restaging scans are due at today's visit/this week (9-month post-olaparib imaging); results pending at the time of this note.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. Metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, germline BRCA1-mutated, on first-line olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg BID -- currently at 6 months of therapy.
   - Disease status: As of last imaging (3 months ago), partial response in liver lesions, stable bone mets. Clinically, the patient continues to feel well without new pain or symptoms concerning for progression. Awaiting results of today's/this week's 9-month restaging CT and bone scan.
   - Continue olaparib 300 mg BID at current dose. No dose modification indicated at this time; anemia does not meet threshold for reduction (Hgb 10.2 g/dL, asymptomatic, threshold is Hgb < 8 g/dL or symptomatic anemia).
   - Will review 9-month imaging at next visit or sooner if results are available and show concerning changes.

2. Anemia, grade 1, macrocytic -- PARP inhibitor-associated (olaparib effect).
   - Hgb 10.2 g/dL with macrocytosis (MCV 98 fL). Iron studies negative for iron deficiency. B12 and folate levels ordered today to exclude concurrent deficiency, though macrocytosis in this context is most consistent with PARP inhibitor effect on erythroid precursors.
   - No evidence of hemolysis. No transfusion indicated at present.
   - Will monitor CBC at next visit in 6-8 weeks.
   - If Hgb drops below 8 g/dL or patient becomes symptomatic, will consider olaparib dose reduction to 250 mg BID per package insert guidance.

3. Bone metastases -- on zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg IV q4 weeks.
   - Last infusion administered on schedule. Tolerating well; no jaw discomfort or dental concerns (dental clearance was completed prior to starting Zometa).
   - Calcium 1200 mg/day and vitamin D 800 IU/day supplementation ongoing.
   - Right 5th rib pain improved to 1/10 following palliative RT; no longer requiring breakthrough opioid. Continue scheduled acetaminophen PRN.

4. Peripheral sensory neuropathy, grade 1, bilateral feet -- residual from prior paclitaxel.
   - Stable on duloxetine 60 mg daily. No progression. Functional impact minimal.
   - Continue current management. No change.

5. Hypothyroidism -- pembrolizumab-associated (irAE from prior adjuvant therapy).
   - TSH 2.4 mIU/L, well-controlled. Continue levothyroxine 50 mcg daily.
   - Recheck TSH in 6 months.

6. Nausea -- grade 1, olaparib-associated.
   - Well-managed with ondansetron 4 mg PRN. Continue as-needed regimen. Counseled to take olaparib with food to mitigate nausea.

7. Risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) -- planned with gynecologic oncology.
   - Patient has scheduled procedure given germline BRCA1 mutation. I communicated with the gyn-onc team regarding timing in context of ongoing olaparib -- plan to hold olaparib perioperatively per surgical team preference, with plan to resume as soon as oral intake is adequate post-op.
   - RRSO will render the patient surgically menopausal; counseled regarding vasomotor symptoms (avoid estrogen given breast cancer history; consider non-hormonal options such as venlafaxine or gabapentin if needed post-operatively). Will coordinate closely with gyn-onc.

8. Psychosocial support.
   - Patient reports manageable anxiety. She was referred to the oncology social worker at a prior visit; follow-up with social work encouraged. Palliative care team is co-managing.
   - Continues to work part-time, which is meaningful to her.

Return to clinic: 6-8 weeks, or sooner if 9-month restaging imaging shows new or progressive findings. Labs at next visit: CBC, CMP, TSH.

---
Electronically signed by: [Attending Oncologist, MD]
Dictated and reviewed by attending.
